---
title: "Track: Kernels & tails"
page-layout: full
---

## When to use this track

Choose this path if your main question is “**what distribution family should I use** for the bulk, and when do I need a GPD tail?”.

## Path (recommended)

1. [Kernels hub](../kernels.qmd)
2. [Kernel catalog](../kernels/kernels-index.qmd)
3. [Summary: distribution/backend reference](../kernels/summary-distribution-backend-reference.qmd)
4. [Intro: GPD kernel and conventions](../kernels/introduction-with-gpd-kernel.qmd)
## Quick decision aids

- Use `kernel_support_table()` to confirm what each kernel supports.
- Use examples as templates to compare kernels on the same data.

<!-- cmgpd-footer:start -->
## Prereqs
- Required packages and data for this page are listed in the setup chunks above.

## Outputs
- This page renders model fits, diagnostics, and summary artifacts generated by package APIs.

## Interpretation
- Canonical concept page: [Introduction With Gpd Kernel](../kernels/introduction-with-gpd-kernel.qmd)
- Treat this page as an application/example view and use the canonical page for core definitions.

## Next
- Continue to the linked canonical concept page, then return for implementation-specific details.
<!-- cmgpd-footer:end -->
